title: The Magic Words
lesson: Kind words like "please" and "thank you" make people smile.

gists: In a cozy home kitchen, the child (far left) sits at a wooden table, cheerful, while the grandmother in the background at right holds a plate of cookies.
In the kitchen, the child (far left) reaches eagerly toward the cookies with eyes fixed on the plate while the grandmother at right holds the plate, watching patiently — the child wants a cookie.
In the kitchen, the child (far left) clasps their hands politely as if saying 'please' with a warm smile while the grandmother at right beams, holding the plate — the child remembers the magic word.
In the kitchen, the child (far left) reaches out with both hands to receive a cookie while the grandmother at right leans in offering the plate — the cookie is given.
In the kitchen, the child (far left) holds a cookie close with both hands as if saying 'thank you', joyful, while the grandmother at right rests a hand on her heart, warm.
In the kitchen in golden light, the child (far left) shares the plate of cookies while the grandmother at right leans close with a hand on their shoulder — cosy, happy, kind words shared.
texts: You sat at the kitchen table. Grandma had a plate of warm cookies. Mmm, they smelled delicious!
You really, really wanted one, and you reached your hand toward the plate.
Then you remembered the magic word. "Please may I have a cookie?" you asked with a smile.
"Of course you may!" said Grandma, and she happily held out the plate.
You took a cookie and hugged it close. "Thank you, Grandma!" Her heart felt warm and glad.
You shared the cookies together, cosy and happy. Kind words made you both smile.
